task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar OS Memorize activity interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the game title to 'South American Capitals'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The activity title in the top toolbar is changed to 'South American Capitals'.

task_completion.description: Begin adding country and capital pairs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Memorize game creation/editing interface is visible, showing at least one or two of the required country-capital pairs entered (e.g., Argentina and Buenos Aires).

task_completion.description: Add exactly 8 specific country and capital pairs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Memorize game creation/editing interface shows exactly 8 pairs containing the specified countries (Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Peru, Uruguay, Venezuela, Ecuador) and their corresponding capitals.

task_completion.description: Ensure the game is saved to the Journal
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar Journal interface is visible showing an entry for 'South American Capitals' with the Memorize activity icon, or the activity is explicitly closed/kept to trigger the save.

integrity.description: Agent used the Memorize GUI to create the game
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the Memorize GUI to add cards, rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to generate the game file.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Memorize application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the genuine Sugar OS Memorize activity, not a text editor or web browser.

integrity.description: Agent manually entered the card data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting the text for the cards into the Memorize interface fields, rather than loading a pre-made game file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Journal Title: 'South American Capitals'. Expected 8 pairs: Argentina/Buenos Aires, Brazil/Brasilia, Chile/Santiago, Colombia/Bogota, Peru/Lima, Uruguay/Montevideo, Venezuela/Caracas, Ecuador/Quito.
